messages.message: I intend to focus on securing southern Europe and protecting my supply centers. If you are moving toward Maroc or the Iberian region, I’d be happy to coordinate to avoid conflict in the Mediterranean.

Hey Italy! You know what they say about Italy/austria: if one of us dies, the other gets screwed over real quick. I'd love to find a way to help one another out this game. If you have any thoughts on openings or strategies, please let me know!
I have no immediate interest in expanding northward into your territory. If you are looking to push east or west, I’d be happy to avoid interference in the short term.
Hi France! As the two western powers we both stand to gain from working together against the rest of the board. Let me know if you are up for working together?
Hey Germany! I'd love to find a way to help one another out this game. If you have any thoughts on openings or strategies, please let me know!

Hey Russia!
I think the RT is probably one of the most underrated alliances on the board, so I hope you're down to give it a try! What are your thoughts on the Black Sea?

Hi Austria! Of course, I think we should consider working together, and would suggest that we stay friendly and share any intel we get. We are both very exposed to an early attack from Russia and Turkey, so are you up for an aggressive alliance to counter them?
Hi England, how's it going? As the two western powers, we both stand to gain from working together. Let me know if you are up for working together?

Yeah I love the RT and agree that it is one of the most underrated alliances on the board. I'm not a big fan of bouncing in Black Sea as it is such a key point of contention between us, and the entire board panics when they see a RT alliance as there is almost no way to hide it.
Hello Italy! I think we should discuss options for working together, and share any intel we get. Interested?

If we can get Austria to open anti-Italian, we can simply take Austrian centers while Austria struggles with Italy.

If we do it right, we could completely dominate this board together and achieve a 17-17. We just need to set up so that I get into Scandinavia and you control the Balkans. This alliance is extremely strong as we can devote our forces to one front.
Hello France! I hope we can be long term allies and coordinate on multiple fronts of this game.
Hi Russia, how's it going? I think we should discuss how we can best work together, and I suggest we share any intel we get. Interested?

Hello England! We are unlikely to be hostile towards each other early in the game. I hope we can achieve peace early and perhaps be long term allies. I won't contest your control of Norway and will refrain from building in the north as long as you respect that claim.
I aim to consolidate my position in Italy and the Mediterranean. If your plans involve the Ionian Sea or Greece, I’d prefer to avoid conflict and discuss mutual goals.

I appreciate your sentiment, and I agree that mutual cooperation can benefit both of us. My immediate focus is on securing southern Europe and protecting my supply centers. If you're considering a push eastward or into the Balkans, I’d be happy to support your efforts while you help me deter Turkey’s expansion into the region.
Hello Turkey! I think we should discuss options for working together, and share any intel we get. Interested?
Hello Austria! I am going to ask that you don't move to Galicia as I have no desire to attack you. I'm thinking of opening north so that I can focus on Scandinavia.
Sounds like a plan! I've gotten Russia's message and he's planning on a northern opening so you'll have an opening to push east, if you want to take it.

I’m open to working together. My goals involve securing southern Europe and maintaining a strong Mediterranean presence. If your plans involve stabilizing central Europe or moving eastward, I’d be happy to coordinate and avoid conflicts along our borders.

If we are working together, and you decide to make a move on Warsaw, I can get in position to support you in. Otherwise I need to bounce him in Sweden, which puts me at odds with Russia in a big way. If we are looking at an all-out fight with Russia, then I don't want to antagonize him unnecessarily. Let me know if you decide to go for Warsaw, and if you do I'll adjust my plans accordingly.
I have no interest in challenging your naval dominance in the north. If your focus is on the North Sea or the Channel, I’d be happy to keep our Mediterranean operations separate and avoid conflict.
Sounds good. I'm still working on figuring out whether I'm going to go for France, England or Russia first, so we can coordinate once I know more.

I am very interested in this idea. How would you feel about allowing me to take Sweden in 1901?

Perhaps, we can convince England to open against France, which would let you snag Belgium uncontested and could leave the two of you in a strong position to attack him in 1902.
Hi Russia! You don't need to ask. I was going to propose an alliance with you. Let me know if you're interested.

Hey Italy,
Obviously my goals are in the Balkans, as are yours. I hope that we can avoid conflict as well. Would you be interested in an alliance against Austria?

Hi Italy, good luck this game!

I agree that we have a lot to benefit from working together, at least as far as intel and ultimately working together against our mutual enemies. Just a heads up, it's usually wise to be on guard against Austria early on, since they tend to dominate games and their only real expansion route is through you and Turkey. Maybe you two have already talked, but that's something to be aware of.

Hey Germany! I definitely agree, a western alliance is pretty much what I was going to suggest. I was thinking the typical split of Bel for me and Hol for you, then we can try to split England up between us?
I'm not moving in this turn but he is planning to move there and asked me not to take Galicia. If you bounce him, I will join in the attack next turn
Perfect. Do you want to DMZ Burgundy? Or should we bounce to mask our intentions?
Hey Italy, that sounds great, I would really appreciate that. I'll be taking Spain and Portugal as my builds, but I think that I'd also prefer if we Dmz'd Piedmont and the Gulf of Lyon
I think that I would prefer a DMZ, since I'm pretty sure I'm going to be using that army to move to Pic in the Autumn and take Bel in the next Spring
I'd be fine with you taking Sweden, if you are moving against Turkey. Is that your plan? If so, I will stay out of your way.
Hey Russia, that sounds great to me, I was going to suggest the same thing
Do you mean move to Silesia, or to Galicia?
My main focus is heading East for now. I believe Germany is going to focus on Russia in the North so if you'd like to take Turkey from the South, I'd be happy to coordinate an attack.
Sure! Although right now there's not a whole lot of Intel to share lol
Ok, DMZ it is.
I'm fine with bouncing him, but only if I know for sure that's what you are doing.
What's your plan with Venice? I'd like to DMZ Tyrolia, so I'd appreciate it if you did the same.
True. Any plans to go after Russia?
sorry my bad, I mean move to galicia. if you bounce him, I will join the fight next turn
Was there anything specific that you had in mind?

Hi Russia, good luck this game!

I appreciate your offer of cooperation and hope that we can both be successful. I also have no interest in contesting your claim to Sweden. May our friendly encounters continue
Ok, I will move to Silesia.
Cool, thanks!

Hi Germany, good luck this game!

I'm certainly open to working together. Are you planning to head east, or are you thinking of moving west?

My immediate focus remains securing southern Europe and the Mediterranean. If your goals involve the Balkans or the Black Sea, I’d prefer to avoid conflict and may be open to coordinating to keep Turkey from expanding westward.
Have you heard anything from England? He didn't respond to my message.

I appreciate your proposal to DMZ the region around Piedmont and the Gulf of Lyon. That sounds reasonable, and I have no interest in expanding westward into your sphere. I’ll ensure my army in Venice holds its position unless you confirm your moves are clear of Tyrolia.
sounds good

Not right now. If we can recruit Germany to open anti-England and make it look like England and I are going to war, it would make it easier for me to get into Scandinavia and you to move into England with German assistance.
I'm not getting a good feeling from France and England. Have you heard anything?

Hi France, good luck this game!

I hope that we can work together. Any thoughts on Belgium? Also, how have your early conversations with Germany gone?

If you're open to it, I'm willing to DMZ the English Channel, allowing you to move into MAO and then potentially into Spain (and Portugal).

An attack on Turkey from the south sounds promising, especially if we coordinate our efforts. I’ll focus on securing the Mediterranean flank while you push eastward. Let me know if you’d like a more detailed plan.
I'm thinking about moving west. Have you heard anything from France?
And what are your thoughts on Burgundy?
No I haven't heard anything
No that isn't my plan at all. I am interested in working with you against England, with France's assistance, and with a 3 way draw with Turkey. Would you be interested in that sort of plan?

I can agree to DMZ Tyrolia as requested. My army in Venice will hold its ground unless I receive further pressure from France or Austria. I suggest we keep communication open as the situation evolves.
Ok, just checking.
Sure, I would be interested in that. I'm a little nervous about France and England though, so if you could encourage them to fight that would be very helpful.
It might be a good idea to offer to help England take France and France to help England take Russia
Sounds good.

Yeah, it seems like England may be absent from the game, so Russia is going to get strong quickly. We need to make sure Italy and Turkey are fighting each other, or they could get out of hand quickly.
That could free up Russia to go hard against one of us.

I appreciate your agreement to DMZ Tyrolia. Given our mutual interests in stability along our borders, I suggest we continue to monitor France's movements closely. If they attempt to push into your region or mine, I’d be happy to coordinate a response.
Glad to hear it!
A Lepanto?
Venice will hold steady unless provoked. I have no interest in moving into Tyrolia or threatening your border. Let’s keep the region stable for now.
Thanks Italy, that sounds good to me.

Hi Austria, good luck this game!

I hope that we can work together. Obviously we don't have much to talk about, but I wanted to open the lines of communication.
I don't see a reason why we can't coordinate! If you get into position to attack Turkey, I am sure we can work together to make a successful stab.
A Lepanto could be a possibility if we coordinate. I’m open to a joint effort against Turkey if it suits both our interests. Let me know your plans.
Venice will hold steady unless provoked. I have no interest in moving into Tyrolia or threatening your border. Let’s keep the region stable for now.
I haven't decided yet. Obviously I can't make any moves on him without your help, so if you can commit to bouncing him in Sweden that'd definitely help

That’s encouraging to hear. If you’re positioning for an attack on Turkey in the Black Sea, I’ll ensure my forces are ready to support a southern push. Let’s coordinate further as the game progresses.
France seems friendly, but England has been quiet. I'm worried he may team up with Russia against me, so I'm hoping you can take advantage if I'm vulnerable in the south.
I have not heard back from France yet, but I did message them.
If a Lepanto-style push against Turkey interests you, let’s plan for it in the coming turns. I’ll maintain pressure on the southern flank to keep Turkey occupied while you prepare from the north.
I'm leaning towards going for France first, are you interested? I was thinking you could get Marseilles and Spain, and I'd get Paris and Brest.
What are you looking for me to do?

I appreciate your concern about England and Russia. While I have no interest in directly targeting France myself, I can see benefit in helping you if your southern flank becomes vulnerable. However, I’d prefer to focus on securing southern Europe and the Mediterranean. If France moves aggressively toward you, I’ll consider holding my forces ready to deter them from overextending.
What's your feeling about England? France seems pretty cagey.

Hi England, good luck to you as well! I would definitely be down to DMZ the English channel, and I've just talked to Germany about splitting the Bel/hol between us, and I think we've both agreed that that's a good plan
Yes, I am going to bounce Russia out of Sweden.

Hi Turkey, good luck this game!

Obviously we're unlikely to interact much in the early game, but I hope that we can work together in the future. Also, just a heads up, it's usually wise to be on guard against Austria. They tend to dominate games and their only real expansion route is through you and Italy. Maybe you and Italy have already talked, but it's something to be aware of.
Yeah, that could be a good idea. I think that I might be able to convince Germany to move in that direction. Let's see how the opening moves go

I appreciate your offer to coordinate against Turkey. My forces will position to pressure Turkey's southern flank, and I suggest we plan a synchronized push in the coming turns. Let me know your intended route through the Black Sea so we can align our efforts.
Hey Austria! Obviously we share two neighbors, so I was hoping we could discuss how to divide the Balkans and possibly coordinate an attack on one of them?

I appreciate your concerns about England and Russia aligning against you. While my focus remains on southern Europe, I can commit to keeping France occupied along the Mediterranean flank to prevent them from reinforcing your southern border. Regarding your proposal to target France, I’m open to coordination—particularly if we can divide their forces. I’ll hold my units in position and await your confirmed moves.

I appreciate your willingness to coordinate. I'll position my forces to pressure Turkey's southern flank, particularly through the Ionian Sea and Apulia region. If you're planning a push through the Black Sea toward Constantinople, let me know your timing so we can synchronize our efforts.
Yeah that sounds like a plan

Sounds good, DMZ it is!

Let me know if I can be of help, especially if you have further plans for Germany or Italy.

I appreciate your proposal to target France, but my focus remains securing southern Europe and the Mediterranean. However, if France moves aggressively toward your southern flank, I can hold my forces ready to deter them from overextending. Regarding Marseilles and Spain, I’d need clarity on how that aligns with my own interests in the region. For now, I’ll maintain pressure on Turkey’s southern flank while keeping France occupied.
I am opening south.
Yeah, definitely

Great to hear you agree on the Lepanto-style push against Turkey. I’ll position my forces to pressure Turkey’s southern flank, particularly in Apulia and the Ionian Sea, while you prepare from the north. Let’s coordinate timing and routes soon.
Cool, I'll let you know!
Hi England, good luck to you too!

Great, I assume your 'definitely' refers to your own plans. I’ll keep my forces in position along the Mediterranean to avoid any accidental clashes. If you’re moving toward the Iberian region or Maroc, please confirm your route so we can ensure mutual clarity.
England messaged me, we had agreed on a DMZ in the English channel

Opening south sounds like a promising move. I’ll position my forces to pressure Turkey’s southern flank, particularly in Apulia and the Ionian Sea, to support your advance. Let me know your timing and direction so we can align our efforts.
Awesome, sounds like a plan
I'm just not sure what I should do with Sevastopol this year. Any thoughts?

Sevastopol is a strong naval position, but I understand the hesitation about how to use it effectively this turn. If you’re aiming to pressure Turkey from the Black Sea, holding Sevastopol as a defensive anchor could help secure your northern flank while you push southward. Alternatively, if you’re moving toward Constantinople, you could consider moving your fleet from Sevastopol to the Black Sea provinces closer to Turkey’s coast to set up a direct advance. Let me know your plan—I’m happy to coordinate my Mediterranean strategy accordingly.
Yeah, I have talked to Italy. And yeah, I'll definitely keep an eye on him, but thanks for the heads up!

Great! I’m excited about the Lepanto-style push against Turkey. I’ll position my army in Venice to prepare for a move into Apulia this turn, which should pressure Turkey’s southern flank. If you’re focusing on the Balkans or the northern approach to Turkey, I suggest we synchronize our timing so we can overwhelm them together. Let me know your next steps!
Hi Turkey! Sounds good! I would prefer to take Serbia and Greece so if you'd like Rumania and Bulgaria, that works well with me
England seems very friendly to me, and France seemed interested in your anti-English idea, but he didn't commit to anything yet.

Understood. I’ll ensure my forces remain focused on the southern flank and avoid any moves that could threaten your plans toward Iberia or Maroc. Let me know if you’d like confirmation on my specific routes for clarity.
I'd prefer taking out Turkey quickly and would prefer going for a Southern Strategy

With Sevastopol, I recommend holding it steady this turn to secure your northern flank. If you're planning a push toward Constantinople or the Black Sea coast, positioning your fleet closer to the Turkish border could set up a strong advance. Meanwhile, I’ll pressure Turkey’s southern flank through Apulia and the Ionian Sea to support your strategy.

Great, let's set up the southern strategy together. I'll move my army from Venice into Apulia this turn to pressure Turkey's southern flank. If you're preparing to push from the Balkans or northern Turkey, let me know your timing so we can coordinate and overwhelm them together.

Ok. I think that sounds like a good plan. It'll be easier to capture North Sea once we have England on the defensive.

Just keep in mind that if France and England gang up on you, England is likely to end up the main beneficiary as I won't be able to help you much against France.
Excellent! I believe Germany is moving East to Russia so that should give us an opening
Great, thanks.
No problem!

That sounds promising—if Germany moves east, it could free up resources for us to focus on Turkey. I’ll move my army from Venice into Apulia this turn to pressure Turkey’s southern flank. Let me know your plans for the Balkans or northern approach so we can synchronize timing and maximize our advantage.

Obviously we don't really have anything to discuss right now, but I was hoping we could keep in touch and share any intel that might be mutually beneficial. I think Turkey and France are good partners mid-game so I hope to work with you down the line!

yes I am
